codeReview.summary: The restore feature implementation matches the Mission recovery contract: stored Mission sessions are augmented from live `droid.load_session` data when available, first render bootstrap is seeded from Mission snapshot data, Mission-specific notifications remain authoritative over generic session updates, missionDir reconciliation applies the documented per-file merge rules, and completion/live-worker state is derived conservatively so validator injection, disk-only recovery, and stale `currentWorkerSessionId` values do not produce false completed or live-worker UI states. Focused tests in `test/sessionStore.test.ts` and `test/rpcNotificationMapping.test.ts` cover the claimed lifecycle and precedence scenarios, and I did not identify a product-code defect within the reviewed scope.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `mission-core-worker` skill currently requires an explicit red-first workflow, but this cross-layer recovery change needed helper extraction before narrow restore/reconciliation tests were practical. The skill should clarify whether that helper-extraction step is an acceptable precursor to the first failing test for restore-heavy work.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: The handoff records `skillFeedback.followedProcedure = false` for steps 3-4, and the transcript skeleton shows implementation/helper work preceding the focused `rpcNotificationMapping` and `sessionStore` test runs.

summary: Scrutiny review passed for `mission-store-restore-and-reconciliation`. The implementation stays within Mission boundaries and correctly covers restore bootstrap, notification/disk precedence, validator-injected running recovery, disk-only recovery, completion gating, and orphan-worker cleanup; no code issues were identified, and one skill-procedure observation was noted for shared-state follow-up.
